title: gh#11: Deps: Make S3 storage support optional in @sms/shared
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/11.
Backlog section: Refactoring / type safety.

## Problem

`@aws-sdk/client-s3` is currently a production dependency of `@sms/shared`, and `packages/shared/src/storage/index.ts` statically imports `S3Storage`. That means every workspace consumer of `@sms/shared` can pull the AWS SDK graph even though S3 is optional and the default deployment uses local filesystem storage.

## Decision

Use the optional-peer + lazy-import approach.

Keep the storage abstraction in `@sms/shared`, but make the AWS SDK dependency optional and load it only when S3 is actually configured.

## Implementation direction

- Move `@aws-sdk/client-s3` out of `@sms/shared.dependencies`.
- Add it to `@sms/shared.peerDependencies` with `peerDependenciesMeta.optional: true`.
- Keep it in `@sms/shared.devDependencies` so shared typecheck/tests still work.
- Add `@aws-sdk/client-s3` to `@sms/api` and `@sms/worker` production dependencies so S3-capable runtime images still include it.
- Refactor `S3Storage` to lazy-load the AWS SDK with `await import('@aws-sdk/client-s3')` from inside the S3 code path. Use type-only imports where useful.
- Keep `createStorageBackend()` returning local storage by default when `MEDIA_STORAGE_BACKEND` is unset or `local`.
- If `MEDIA_STORAGE_BACKEND=s3` but the optional dependency is unavailable, throw a clear configuration error naming `@aws-sdk/client-s3` and the S3 backend.

## Acceptance criteria

- `@sms/shared` no longer has `@aws-sdk/client-s3` in production `dependencies`.
- Local-storage deployments do not import or initialize the AWS SDK.
- S3 deployments still work when API/worker runtime deps include `@aws-sdk/client-s3`.
- `pnpm --filter @sms/shared typecheck` passes.
- `pnpm --filter @sms/shared test -- storage` passes, or the closest focused storage test command.
- API and worker builds still pass after dependency changes.

## Out of scope

- Moving storage ownership out of `@sms/shared`.
- Removing S3 support.
- Changing the `StorageBackend` public contract except where #27 adds lifecycle cleanup.

## References

- Related lifecycle/storage contract cleanup: #27
- Related storage tests: #28
reproduction: `@aws-sdk/client-s3` is currently a production dependency of `@sms/shared`, and `packages/shared/src/storage/index.ts` statically imports `S3Storage`. That means every workspace consumer of `@sms/shared` can pull the AWS SDK graph even though S3 is optional and the default deployment uses local filesystem storage.
recommendation: Implement the fix described by GitHub issue gh#11 and satisfy its acceptance criteria.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Imported from GitHub issue. Preserve or add focused regression coverage when the issue acceptance criteria calls for it.
